question: Ben can choose 2 of his 4 best friends to go with him to the movies. His best friends are Carla, David, Emma, and Frank. How many different pairs of 2 friends can he choose from the 4?

solution.strategy: Number of ways to choose 2 from n is C(n,2) = n(n-1)/2.

question: Chris can choose 3 of his 5 best friends to form a study group. His best friends are Dana, Evan, Fiona, Greg, and Helen. How many different groups of 3 friends can he choose from the 5?

solution.strategy: Number of ways to choose 3 from n is C(n,3) = n(n-1)(n-2)/6.
